pr.title: Match itoctarockstone and cleanup it_27CF to match it, rename to ityoshiegglay, rename it_2F2B to ityoshitongue
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched the Octarock stone item unit by implementing it_802E89D0 and updating configure.py from NonMatching to Matching for itoctarockstone.c. The PR also renamed generic item units it_27CF and it_2F2B to Yoshi-specific names ityoshiegglay and ityoshitongue, deleted the old it_27CF header/dox, added a focused ityoshiegglay.h, and cleaned many fighter includes to depend on specific item headers instead of the broad it_27CF.h. Some generated matching reports showed apparent broken matches for the renamed units, but the PR title and configure.py indicate these files were reorganized and marked matching under their new names.
what_changed: configure.py changed itoctarockstone.c from NonMatching to Matching.
configure.py replaced item paths it_2F2B.c and it_27CF.c with ityoshitongue.c and ityoshiegglay.c, both listed as Matching.
src/melee/it/items/itoctarockstone.c gained a full implementation of it_802E89D0, replacing a decomp placeholder.
itoctarockstone.c added ft/ftlib.h and baselib/random.h includes and removed the generic it/items/forward.h include.

it_802E89D0 computes an Octarock stone projectile velocity from horizontal speed, item facing direction, nearest fighter position via ftLib_8008627C and ftLib_800866DC, item fall speed, random vertical offset via HSD_Randf, and min/max vertical velocity clamps.
src/melee/it/items/it_27CF.c was renamed to src/melee/it/items/ityoshiegglay.c with its include changed from it_27CF.h to ityoshiegglay.h.
src/melee/it/items/it_2F2B.c was renamed to src/melee/it/items/ityoshitongue.c with its include changed from it_2F2B.h to ityoshitongue.h.
A new src/melee/it/items/ityoshiegglay.h was added with prototypes for the former it_27CF functions and ItemStateTable it_803F94A0.

Several helper wrapper functions that called it_8026B724 or it_8026B73C were removed from ityoshiegglay.c and their declarations appear to have been moved to more specific item headers such as itfoxblaster.h, itgamewatchmanhole.h, and itgamewatchparachute.h.

Fighter source files were updated to include the specific item headers they actually use, such as itfoxblaster.h, itgamewatchmanhole.h, itgamewatchparachute.h, itgamewatchjudge.h, ityoshiegglay.h, and ityoshitongue.h, instead of it_27CF.h.
Large include lists in Kirby special-N related files were trimmed, especially ftKb_SpecialN.c, ftKb_SpecialNFx.c, and ftKb_SpecialNYs.c.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt converted several it_804DD838/83C/840/848 and it_804DDA18/1C/20/28 global sdata2 symbols into local @-numbered float/double labels.
src/melee/it/it_26B1.c received naming/include cleanup, including renaming a local bobOmbGObj variable to gobj in it_8026BE84 and adding specific item headers for heart, linkbomb, and tomato.
smart_moves: Matched itoctarockstone by implementing only the missing projectile velocity routine rather than destabilizing the whole item unit.
Used a small static inline absolute-difference helper in itoctarockstone.c, likely to shape the generated code while keeping the source readable.
Kept vertical velocity calculation explicit and clamped after adding randomization, preserving behavior boundaries in a matching-sensitive routine.
Replaced broad, misleading it_27CF.h dependencies with item-specific headers, reducing accidental cross-item coupling.
Renamed anonymous-numbered item files to gameplay-meaningful names once their identities were clear: ityoshiegglay and ityoshitongue.
Moved exposed prototypes into focused headers, allowing fighter code to include only the item interfaces it uses.
Marked renamed Yoshi item units as Matching in configure.py, keeping the build configuration aligned with file identity changes.
Changed local-only sdata2 constants in symbols.txt from global it_804Dxxxx symbols to local @ labels, matching the way the decompiled code now references constants.
decomp_lessons: When an item file is identified, rename the translation unit and update configure.py at the same time so matching status tracks the real source file.
After removing a catch-all header, expect many fighter files to need direct includes for the item headers that actually provide their prototypes.
A decomp.dev report may show many broken matches after renames because the tracked unit names changed; interpret these reports with file renames and configure.py updates in mind.
For projectile matching, preserving exact arithmetic order, temporary variables, and helper functions can matter as much as the high-level formula.
If constants are only local to a matching unit, updating symbols.txt to local @ labels can be part of getting data matching correct.
Include cleanup can be bundled with item renames when it removes stale dependencies and clarifies ownership of item functions.
Deleting old .dox and header files prevents stale anonymous item names from continuing to appear in future fighter or item work.
naming_conventions: Use item file names that describe the actual item when known, e.g. it_27CF -> ityoshiegglay and it_2F2B -> ityoshitongue.
Keep item headers parallel to item C filenames, e.g. ityoshiegglay.c with ityoshiegglay.h and ityoshitongue.c with ityoshitongue.h.
Generic address-style function names were retained where no better symbol evidence was shown, e.g. it_802F2F34 and it_802F3020 in ityoshiegglay.h.
Existing motion callback names for the renamed Yoshi egg-lay item still use the old it_27CF prefix, showing that file-level naming can improve before every internal symbol is renamed.
Use simple local variable names such as gobj for generic HSD_GObj pointers when the object is not specifically a Bob-omb object.
For local sdata2 constants in symbols.txt, use @number labels rather than exported it_804Dxxxx names when evidence says scope is local.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented it_802E89D0 with explicit temporaries: gp, pos, x, speed, and new_var.
Computed horizontal velocity as horiz_speed multiplied by ip->facing_dir and explicitly zeroed z velocity.
Found a target fighter with ftLib_8008627C and retrieved its position with ftLib_800866DC before computing projectile vertical velocity.
Used an inline absolute-difference helper getX instead of a library fabs call, likely matching original codegen and avoiding unwanted library calls.
Calculated speed as horizontal distance divided by horiz_speed, then used item fall speed and speed squared to derive y velocity.
Introduced HSD_Randf for randomized vertical adjustment based on target/item y difference divided by 10.0f.
Clamped ip->x40_vel.y against max_vy and min_vy and returned early when a target fighter was found; otherwise set vertical velocity to 0.0f.
Changed sdata2 symbols from global labels to local @ labels around 0x804DD838-0x804DD848 and 0x804DDA18-0x804DDA28, consistent with local constant matching.
Preserved source-level matching quirks elsewhere, including an existing comment in ftKb_SpecialN.c noting a comma operator required for regalloc.
Formatting-only changes in fighter files were kept small, such as line wrapping Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls and cast spacing.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported 2 new matches for main/melee/it/items/itoctarockstone: it_802E89D0 reached 100.00% for +268 bytes and .sdata2 reached 100.00% for +5 bytes.

The same bot report listed 21 broken matches for old units main/melee/it/items/it_27CF and main/melee/it/items/it_2F2B, which is consistent with the PR renaming those units rather than necessarily indicating functional regressions.
The bot summary showed matched code 55.09% (-0.03%, -1264 bytes), linked code 31.47% (+0.02%, +676 bytes), matched data 34.55% (-0.00%, -48 bytes), and linked data 28.94% (+0.00%, +32 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Check whether internal it_27CF_* symbols in ityoshiegglay.h/c were later renamed to ityoshiegglay_* after this file rename.
Look for later PRs that address the decomp.dev broken-match accounting for renamed it_27CF and it_2F2B units.
Search for other anonymous item headers that act as broad dependency buckets and can be replaced with item-specific headers.
Inspect later itoctarockstone.c changes for refinements to getX, HSD_Randf usage, or projectile velocity matching.
Check whether ityoshitongue.h received symbol/name cleanup beyond the file-level rename.
